label: Find a service
description: Help users discover and navigate to the right government service through search, browsing, or direct referral.
use_when: Users need to locate the correct government service from multiple available services.
The service has a public-facing catalogue or portal.
do_not_use_when: The user has arrived directly at a specific service via a direct link or notification.
Only a single service exists with no discovery needed.

steps.label: Discover services
steps.description: User browses or searches the catalogue, or arrives via search engine, poster, or referral.

steps.label: Confirm service relevance
steps.description: User reaches the service sheet and confirms they have found the right service.

ux_considerations.text: All discovery routes (search, browse, direct link, kiosk, poster) must lead to the same service sheet.
Discovery should end and the service journey should begin as soon as the user selects a service.

technical_notes.text: The service catalogue must be machine-readable to support agentic service discovery.
